title: Fresh Fried Vietnamese Fromage-Blanc Bun Rieu
description: A kid-friendly Vietnamese dish featuring fromage-blanc, wood-fired-bake, and a dash of flavor, all wrapped up in a delicious bun-rieu.

ingredients.name: Fish Sauce
ingredients.notes: nam pla or other Vietnamese fish sauce

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the fromage-blanc, ground pork, crab meat, onion, ginger, tomato paste, fish sauce, sugar, egg, salt, and black pepper. Mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed. Cover the mixture and let it rest at room temperature for 10 minutes.
Dust a clean surface with rice flour and divide the mixture into 8-10 equal portions, depending on how large you want your buns to be.
Shape each portion into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape. Place the disks onto a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Place the baking sheet into the preheated wood-fired oven and bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the buns are golden brown and crispy.
While the buns are baking, heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Once the oil is hot, add 2-3 buns to the skillet (do not overcrowd). Fry for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crispy.
Repeat with the remaining buns. Serve the fried buns hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
To assemble the bun-rieu, place a few pieces of the fried bun on a plate, add a spoonful of the fromage-blanc mixture on top, and garnish with fresh herbs such as cilantro or scallions.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
